title: Python Basics on Linux Terminal
description: Learn Python fundamentals by running Python directly in the Linux terminal

tasks.title: Welcome to Python
tasks.slide_context: Python is one of the most popular programming languages in the world. It is used for web development, data science, artificial intelligence, automation, and much more. In this session, we will learn Python basics by running code directly in the Linux terminal. You do not need any special software — Python comes pre-installed on Linux. Let us get started.

tasks.title: Open Terminal and Start Python
tasks.sonic_prompt: First, I will open the Linux terminal and start the Python interpreter. Watch the screen carefully.

subtasks.title: Open the Linux Terminal
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Let me open the terminal for you. This is where we will run all our Python code.

subtasks.title: Start Python interpreter
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Now I will start the Python interpreter by typing python3. You will see the Python prompt which looks like three greater-than signs.

tasks.title: Python Basics Part 1: Print and Variables
tasks.sonic_prompt: Now let us learn the first two Python basics — printing text and creating variables.

subtasks.title: Print Hello World
subtasks.sonic_prompt: The first thing every programmer learns is how to print text. The print function displays text on the screen. Watch as I type print hello world.

subtasks.title: Create a variable
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Next, let us create a variable. A variable is like a box that stores a value. I will create a variable called name and set it to your name.

subtasks.title: Print the variable
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Now let us print the variable. Notice I do not use quotes around the variable name — that tells Python to use the value stored inside it.

tasks.title: Python Basics Part 2: Math and Numbers
tasks.sonic_prompt: Now let us learn how Python handles numbers and math operations.

subtasks.title: Basic arithmetic
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Python can do math just like a calculator. Watch as I add two numbers together.

subtasks.title: Multiplication and division
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Now let me show you multiplication using the asterisk symbol and division using the forward slash.

subtasks.title: Store result in a variable
subtasks.sonic_prompt: You can also store the result of a calculation in a variable. Let me calculate the area of a rectangle with width 5 and height 10.

subtasks.title: Print the result
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Now let us print the area using an f-string, which lets us mix text and variables together.

tasks.title: Python Basics Part 3: Lists and Loops
tasks.sonic_prompt: Now we will learn about lists and loops — two of the most powerful features in Python.

subtasks.title: Create a list
subtasks.sonic_prompt: A list is a collection of items. I will create a list of fruits. Lists are defined using square brackets.

subtasks.title: Print the list
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Let us print the entire list to see all the items.

subtasks.title: Loop through the list
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Now I will use a for loop to go through each item in the list and print it one by one. This is how you process every item in a collection.

params.cmd:
for fruit in fruits:
    print(f'I like {fruit}')

tasks.title: Python Basics Part 4: If Statements
tasks.sonic_prompt: Finally, let us learn about if statements — how Python makes decisions.

subtasks.title: Simple if statement
subtasks.sonic_prompt: An if statement checks a condition and runs code only if the condition is true. Let me check if a number is greater than ten.

subtasks.title: If-else statement
subtasks.sonic_prompt: We can also add an else block that runs when the condition is false. Let me check if a number is even or odd.

subtasks.title: Exit Python
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Great work! We have covered all five Python basics — print, variables, math, lists with loops, and if statements. Let me exit the Python interpreter now.

tasks.title: Session Complete
tasks.slide_context: Congratulations! You have completed the Python Basics session. Today you learned five fundamental Python concepts: printing output with the print function, creating and using variables, performing math operations, working with lists and for loops, and making decisions with if statements. These are the building blocks of every Python program. Keep practicing and you will become a confident Python developer in no time.
